# Chrome Web Store Listing

## Name
gTabs — AI Tab Organizer

## Short Description (≤132 chars)
One-click AI tab organizer. Groups your chaos into color-coded sections. Review suggestions, apply, undo. Works with 8 LLM providers.

## Detailed Description

**Your tabs are a mess. One click fixes that.**

gTabs uses any LLM to read your open tabs and group them intelligently into color-coded sections — Dev Tools, Research, Shopping, whatever makes sense. You review editable suggestions before anything changes.

**Core workflow**
- Click ✦ Organize All → AI suggests groups
- Rename groups, change colors, remove any you don't want
- Click Apply — done. Undo with one click if needed.

**Smart features**
- Zero-LLM Fast Routing — new tabs are instantly routed into existing groups using domain history, no API calls
- Domain Rules — always put github.com in Dev, always. Hard-wired, zero latency.
- Merge mode — only organize ungrouped tabs, never touch groups you've already made
- Duplicate finder — spot and close duplicate tabs across your window
- Focus Group — collapse everything except your current group
- Sort Groups — alphabetically sort groups by domain

**8 LLM providers, most with free tiers**
- Groq (free, fast)
- Grok / xAI ($25 free credit)
- OpenRouter (free models)
- Ollama (local, free)
- Chrome Built-in AI (local, free)
- Anthropic, OpenAI, or any custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint

**Privacy**
- Zero runtime dependencies — no SDKs, no tracking, no third-party scripts
- API keys stored locally in chrome.storage only
- Tab titles and URLs never leave your machine except the AI call you explicitly trigger
- Telemetry is operator-configured at build time and off by default

**Open source**
github.com/vaddisrinivas/gtabs

## Category
Productivity

## Language
English

## Privacy Policy
gTabs does not collect, transmit, or sell any personal data. Tab URLs and titles are sent only to the LLM provider you configure. API keys are stored locally. See README for full details.
